mots.sens: We breathed into her of Our Spirit

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ع ل م

versets.ar: إِنَّ هَٰذِهِ أُمَّتُكُمْ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً وَأَنَا رَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُونِ
versets.tr: inna hādhihi ummatukum ummatan wāḥidatan wa-anā rabbukum fa-ʿbudūni
versets.trad: Indeed, this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all of you, I created; you all agree in poverty, in weakness, in need. “And I am your Lord” — your Creator, under the attribute of oneness. The community of the prophets is one, because the object of their faith is one. The oneness of the Lord founds the oneness of religion: all the messengers call to the same One, and all men share the same destitution before Him.

mots.racine: أ م م / و ح د
ر ب ب / ع ب د
mots.sens: I am your Lord, so worship Me

versets.ar: وَتَقَطَّعُوا أَمْرَهُم بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ كُلٌّ إِلَيْنَا رَاجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-taqaṭṭaʿū amrahum baynahum kullun ilaynā rājiʿūna
versets.trad: But they fragmented their affair among themselves; all will return to Us.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they diverged and quarreled, their affairs were troubled, their states scattered — and the trials uprooted them. The rupture of the original unity is the source of disorder: to divide over religion is to give oneself over to chaos. But the return restores all: “all return to Us” — how should they not, they who move only within the grip of the decree?

mots.racine: ق ط ع / أ م ر
mots.sens: they fragmented their affair among themselves

mots.racine: ر ج ع

versets.ar: فَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِنَ الصَّالِحَاتِ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَا كُفْرَانَ لِسَعْيِهِ وَإِنَّا لَهُ كَاتِبُونَ
versets.tr: fa-man yaʿmal mina ṣ-ṣāliḥāti wa-huwa muʾminun fa-lā kufrāna li-saʿyihi wa-innā lahu kātibūna
versets.trad: Whoever does righteous deeds, being a believer, his striving will not be denied; We are recording it for him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever takes trouble for God does not lose with God; whoever bears for God a hardship, his due is binding. And he meditates on “being a believer”: this qualification concerns the outcome and the end; one may do beautiful deeds without his end being sealed with felicity — he is a believer in the moment, but without reward; and only if his end is upon Islam and tawḥīd is his striving not lost. The faith that crowns the deed gives it its eternal worth.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح / أ م ن
mots.sens: he does righteous deeds being a believer

mots.racine: ك ف ر / س ع ي

versets.ar: وَحَرَامٌ عَلَىٰ قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْنَاهَا أَنَّهُمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḥarāmun ʿalā qaryatin ahlaknāhā annahum lā yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: It is forbidden for a town We have destroyed ever to return,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We annihilate a people, even obstinate in disobedience, only when We know that they will not believe and that their affair is sealed with misery (shaqāwa). Destruction does not precede God's knowledge of their sealed heart; it follows it. The irreversibility of the punishment is but the reflection of the irreversibility of their refusal: they will not return, for they never wished to return.

mots.racine: ح ر م / ق ر ي
mots.sens: it is forbidden for a town

mots.racine: ر ج ع

versets.ar: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا فُتِحَتْ يَأْجُوجُ وَمَأْجُوجُ وَهُم مِّن كُلِّ حَدَبٍ يَنسِلُونَ
versets.tr: ḥattā idhā futiḥat yaʾjūju wa-maʾjūju wa-hum min kulli ḥadabin yansilūna
versets.trad: until, when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they swarm down from every height.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: that is: until the decreed word is fulfilled against them and the appointed term is completed; then their days will appear, and no salvation of men from their evil will come about except to the measure known in the decree. The bursting forth of Gog and Magog is a sign of the Hour: the swarming of the multitudes “from every height” announces the approach of the end, in the exact order that predestination has set.

mots.racine: ف ت ح
mots.sens: until Gog and Magog are opened

mots.racine: ح د ب / ن س ل

versets.ar: وَاقْتَرَبَ الْوَعْدُ الْحَقُّ فَإِذَا هِيَ شَاخِصَةٌ أَبْصَارُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَا وَيْلَنَا قَدْ كُنَّا فِي غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ هَٰذَا بَلْ كُنَّا ظَالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qtaraba l-waʿdu l-ḥaqqu fa-idhā hiya shākhiṣatun abṣāru lladhīna kafarū yā waylanā qad kunnā fī ghaflatin min hādhā bal kunnā ẓālimīna
versets.trad: And the true promise draws near, and behold, the eyes of the disbelievers stare in horror: “Woe to us! We were heedless of this; nay, we were wrongdoers!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Resurrection seizes them unexpectedly, the signs of the Hour appear suddenly; the liars acknowledge that the fault is theirs — but at a moment when no excuse is any longer accepted, at an hour when their faith no longer avails them. The eye “fixed” (shākhiṣ) in shock tells of the irruption of evidence too late. The sūra that opened on heedlessness (ghafla) returns to it: they confess their ghafla at last, but the confession has lost its price.

mots.racine: ق ر ب / و ع د / ح ق ق
ش خ ص / ب ص ر

versets.ar: إِنَّكُمْ وَمَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ حَصَبُ جَهَنَّمَ أَنتُمْ لَهَا وَارِدُونَ
versets.tr: innakum wa-mā taʿbudūna min dūni llāhi ḥaṣabu jahannama antum lahā wāridūna
versets.trad: You and what you worship besides God are fuel for Hell; to it you will come.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes a grammatical subtlety: “what you worship” (mā), not “whom” (man) — so the stone idols, not the angels whom some worshipped, nor Jesus whom others worshipped; for He says “you and mā” and not “and man.” And he adds: the idols are inert, with no body that suffers; their burning is no punishment for them, but a declaration of their innocence — the sin is the disbelievers', and the idols are mere matter.

mots.racine: ع ب د
ح ص ب / ج ه ن م
mots.def: ḥaṣab: what is thrown into fire.

versets.ar: لَوْ كَانَ هَٰؤُلَاءِ آلِهَةً مَّا وَرَدُوهَا ۖ وَكُلٌّ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: law kāna hāʾulāʾi ālihatan mā waradūhā wa-kullun fīhā khālidūna
versets.trad: Had these been gods, they would not have come to it; but all will abide therein forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the people said: “we worship them only that they may bring us nearer to God”; they knew the idols were inert, but imagined they had some rank with God. God shows them — tomorrow — that if they deserved worship, if they had any rank with Him, they would not be cast into the fire nor burned. The proof is in the fire: what was worshipped enters it like all the rest, forever belying its supposed divinity.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
و ر د
mots.sens: they would not be led to it
mots.def: warada: to arrive at water, at fire.

versets.ar: لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌ وَهُمْ فِيهَا لَا يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: lahum fīhā zafīrun wa-hum fīhā lā yasmaʿūna
versets.trad: For them therein is groaning, and therein they will hear nothing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī details: “for them” — the idol-worshippers; “therein” — in the fire; “a groaning” (zafīr) of regret over what they have lost; “they do not hear” — no call to announce the end of their pain. And he sets against it, full of hope: conversely, the disobedient Muslims in the fire, though punished for a time, will one day hear the word announcing the end of their torment — even after long duration. The silence of despair is only for one who has refused the One.

mots.racine: ز ف ر
س م ع

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ سَبَقَتْ لَهُم مِّنَّا الْحُسْنَىٰ أُولَٰئِكَ عَنْهَا مُبْعَدُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna sabaqat lahum minnā l-ḥusnā ulāʾika ʿanhā mubʿadūna
versets.trad: Those for whom the best [reward] from Us has gone before — they will be kept far from it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “the best [promise] that has gone before them from Us”: the word of goodness, the will and desire of goodness — for the best is His act; and “has gone before” informs of His eternity: what they had in eternity is the word, an attribute attached to them in the sense of the announcement of felicity. And finely: He says “kept far” (mubʿadūn) in the passive, not “keeping far” (mutabāʿidūn), so that the learned may know that all rests on the decree and God's prior judgment, not on the servant's distancing or drawing near.

mots.racine: س ب ق / ح س ن
ب ع د
mots.sens: those are kept far from it

versets.ar: لَا يَسْمَعُونَ حَسِيسَهَا ۖ وَهُمْ فِي مَا اشْتَهَتْ أَنفُسُهُمْ خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: lā yasmaʿūna ḥasīsahā wa-hum fī mā shtahat anfusuhum khālidūna
versets.trad: They will not hear its faintest sound, and they will abide forever in what their souls desired.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this shows that they are punished in nothing; it concerns the believing servants who have no fault. Not to hear even the “crackle” (ḥasīs) of the fire: the distance is so total that no echo of Hell reaches them. And “abiding forever in what their souls desire”: dwelling without ever leaving. The height of bliss is this fulfilled and perpetual desire, in a peace that no sound of torment disturbs.

mots.racine: س م ع / ح س س
ش ه و / ن ف س / خ ل د
mots.sens: abiding in what their souls desire

versets.ar: لَا يَحْزُنُهُمُ الْفَزَعُ الْأَكْبَرُ وَتَتَلَقَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ هَٰذَا يَوْمُكُمُ الَّذِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ
versets.tr: lā yaḥzunuhumu l-fazaʿu l-akbaru wa-tatalaqqāhumu l-malāʾikatu hādhā yawmukumu lladhī kuntum tūʿadūna
versets.trad: The greatest terror will not grieve them, and the angels will meet them: “This is your Day, which you were promised!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports several meanings of “the greatest terror” (al-fazaʿ al-akbar): the angel's word “no good tidings this Day for the criminals”; or “stand apart this Day, O criminals”; or the announcement to the people of Paradise of an eternity without death, and to the people of the fire of an eternity without death; or “remain in it despised and do not speak to Me.” And he adds: it is also said that the greatest terror is separation, or the despair of God's mercy. To the elect, the angels come forward — an intimate welcome at the threshold of the promised Day.

mots.racine: ح ز ن / ف ز ع / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the greatest terror grieves them not

mots.racine: ل ق ي / م ل ك

versets.ar: يَوْمَ نَطْوِي السَّمَاءَ كَطَيِّ السِّجِلِّ لِلْكُتُبِ ۚ كَمَا بَدَأْنَا أَوَّلَ خَلْقٍ نُّعِيدُهُ ۚ وَعْدًا عَلَيْنَا ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا فَاعِلِينَ
versets.tr: yawma naṭwī s-samāʾa ka-ṭayyi s-sijilli lil-kutubi kamā badaʾnā awwala khalqin nuʿīduhu waʿdan ʿalaynā innā kunnā fāʿilīna
versets.trad: The Day We shall roll up the heaven as a scroll rolls up writings. As We began the first creation, We shall repeat it — a promise binding upon Us. We shall surely do it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the rolling-up of the sky a meditation on separation: the sky was a raised roof only while the saints were beneath it, and the earth a carpet while they were upon it; when the beloved depart, their dwellings fall to ruin — as is the custom of men: dwellings fall to ruin after the departure of the beloved. And he adds: or We roll up the sky to bring near to the beloved the distances to be traversed. The recreation, identical to the first, is a promise that God owes Himself to fulfill.

mots.racine: ط و ي / س ج ل
mots.sens: We roll up the sky as a scroll

mots.racine: ب د أ / خ ل ق / ع و د
mots.sens: as We began, We repeat it
mots.def: badaʾa: to begin; aʿāda: to recreate.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ كَتَبْنَا فِي الزَّبُورِ مِن بَعْدِ الذِّكْرِ أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ يَرِثُهَا عِبَادِيَ الصَّالِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad katabnā fī z-zabūri min baʿdi dh-dhikri anna l-arḍa yarithuhā ʿibādiya ṣ-ṣāliḥūna
versets.trad: We have written in the Psalms (al-Zabūr), after the Reminder, that the earth shall be inherited by My righteous servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī specifies: “the Reminder” here is the Torah; “We have written” means: We have announced and decreed; and “the righteous” are the community of Muḥammad — they are the ones who shall inherit the earth. The promise is ancient, inscribed in the earlier Scriptures: the earth returns to those worthy of it through righteousness. The spiritual inheritance precedes and founds the earthly inheritance of the just servants.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ز ب ر
أ ر ض / و ر ث / ص ل ح
mots.sens: the earth, My righteous servants inherit it

versets.ar: إِنَّ فِي هَٰذَا لَبَلَاغًا لِّقَوْمٍ عَابِدِينَ
versets.tr: inna fī hādhā la-balāghan li-qawmin ʿābidīna
versets.trad: In this is a sufficient message for a worshipping people.
versets.commentaire: The “balāgh” is the message that suffices, the announcement that reaches its goal. The Qurʾān carries within itself enough to guide “the people who worship” — those whose heart is turned toward service. The sūra that has unrolled the procession of the prophets arrives at this realization: all of it is an accomplished message for whoever would serve God. To the worshipper, the revealed indication suffices; he needs no further wonder.

mots.racine: ب ل غ
mots.sens: in this is a sufficient message

mots.racine: ع ب د

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnāka illā raḥmatan lil-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: We have sent you only as a mercy to the worlds.
versets.commentaire: The summit of the sūra, and of all prophecy. Qushayrī: as for the one who embraces Islam, it is through you that he is saved; and as for the one who disbelieves, We do not punish him as long as you are among them. You are therefore a mercy from Us upon all creatures together. The Prophet's mercy overflows the community of believers: his very presence is grace for all, believers and deniers alike, a veil of clemency spread over the worlds.

mots.racine: ر س ل
ر ح م / ع ل م
mots.sens: except as a mercy to the worlds

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّمَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ أَنَّمَا إِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ ۖ فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul innamā yūḥā ilayya annamā ilāhukum ilāhun wāḥidun fa-hal antum muslimūna
versets.trad: Say: “It is only revealed to me that your God is one God. Will you then submit?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the oneness in stages: One in His essence, One in His attributes, One in His acts; One without portion, One without likeness, One without partner. And he interprets “will you submit?”: sincere in the covenant of tawḥīd, by disavowing all other in the illusion that it could count for divinity? True submission (islām) is not only to acknowledge the One, but to wash oneself of all attachment to other than Him.

mots.racine: أ ل ه / و ح د
س ل م
mots.def: muslim: one who surrenders to God.

versets.ar: فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَقُلْ آذَنتُكُمْ عَلَىٰ سَوَاءٍ ۖ وَإِنْ أَدْرِي أَقَرِيبٌ أَم بَعِيدٌ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ
versets.tr: fa-in tawallaw fa-qul ādhantukum ʿalā sawāʾin wa-in adrī a-qarībun am baʿīdun mā tūʿadūna
versets.trad: If they turn away, say: “I have warned you all alike, though I do not know whether what you are promised is near or far.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they turn away and do not believe, say: by obligation I have informed you; the argument has turned against you, and the right way has grown obscure to your eyes. And on ignorance of the term: my knowledge is too short for the detail of your states in your becoming and the time of what you are promised; but God's judgment does not tarry when He wills to change your states. The Prophet warns in full equity, without claiming to know the hour, which belongs to God alone.

mots.racine: أ ذ ن / س و ي
mots.def: ādhana: to make known; sawāʾ: equally.

mots.racine: ق ر ب / ب ع د / و ع د
mots.sens: near or far what you are promised

versets.ar: إِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْجَهْرَ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْتُمُونَ
versets.tr: innahu yaʿlamu l-jahra mina l-qawli wa-yaʿlamu mā taktumūna
versets.trad: He knows what is spoken aloud and He knows what you conceal.
themes: the knowledge of the open and the hidden
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: none hides from Him your secret nor your counsel, your state nor your becoming, your outward nor your inward; according to the measure of what you deserve He recompenses you, and according to what your acts require He reckons and repays you. Divine knowledge embraces equally the proclaimed and the concealed: nothing of the without or the within escapes Him. Before this total gaze, the recompense will be of exact justice.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ج ه ر / ق و ل
ك ت م
mots.sens: and He knows what you conceal

versets.ar: وَإِنْ أَدْرِي لَعَلَّهُ فِتْنَةٌ لَّكُمْ وَمَتَاعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-in adrī laʿallahu fitnatun lakum wa-matāʿun ilā ḥīnin
versets.trad: I do not know — perhaps it is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a time.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: my knowledge embraces only what He teaches me; and His teaching is not at my choice, nor willed according to my desire and preference. The Prophet himself knows of the divine design only what is unveiled to him: the respite granted to the deniers is perhaps a trial (fitna), perhaps a brief enjoyment. To acknowledge one's ignorance of the hidden design is the very humility of the servant, be he the Messenger.

mots.racine: ف ت ن
م ت ع / ح ي ن

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ احْكُم بِالْحَقِّ ۗ وَرَبُّنَا الرَّحْمَٰنُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi ḥkum bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-rabbunā r-raḥmānu l-mustaʿānu ʿalā mā taṣifūna
versets.trad: Say: “My Lord, judge by the Truth! Our Lord is the All-Merciful, the One whose help is sought against what you ascribe.”
versets.commentaire: The sūra seals on the appeal to the Truth and recourse to the All-Merciful. Qushayrī: the All-Merciful (al-Raḥmān) is the abundant in mercy, general to each one; and from Him comes the help and aid, when it comes and how it comes. After the whole unfolding of denial and trial, the last word belongs to mercy: it is to the Raḥmān that help is asked, it is by the Truth that He will judge. The very Name that opened the basmala closes the sūra of the Prophets.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ح ك م / ح ق ق
mots.sens: my Lord, judge by the Truth

mots.racine: ر ح م / ع و ن
mots.sens: the All-Merciful whose help is sought
mots.def: mustaʿān: the one whose aid is implored.
